commands.review_summary:
This PR quarantines unreadable or unsupported runtime tool schemas before provider/model projection across embedded attempts, compaction, and doctor runtime checks, with focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +315, Tests +413. Total +728 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and the branch's focused tests: unreadable or non-serializable tool schemas are read before provider/model projection and the PR exercises those paths. I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve quarantined readable names in bundled runtime reservations; maintainer review is still needed for the compatibility policy and stacked-base landing.

target.title: fix(agents): quarantine invalid runtime tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- Quarantines malformed runtime tool schemas before provider/model projection so one bad active tool cannot crash an assistant turn before content.
- Applies the same fail-closed path to normal embedded attempts and compaction runs, including unreadable tool rows and provider-normalization-only hazards.
- Emits runtime diagnostics for quarantined tools and shares the readable-tool/quarantine helpers so the hardening does not duplicate defensive logic across runner paths.

## Verification

- Rebuilt the PR on refreshed fixture base `fee8f709550`; pushed head `17132fa241a`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: passed, 3 routed shards / 125 tests.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...`: passed on touched runtime/test files.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...`: passed on touched runtime/test files.
- `git diff --check @{u}...HEAD`: passed.
- Touched-file scrub found no reported private plugin/tool name or private path/network literals.
- `node scripts/changed-lanes.mjs --json` and `node scripts/check-changed.mjs --dry-run`: selected `core`, `coreTests`, and `extensionTests`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base @{u}`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Blacksmith Testbox path was blocked by missing local auth: `not authenticated — run 'blacksmith auth login' first`.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate `run_2e97ba819a4b` / `cbx_7febafe69340` ran `pnpm check:changed`: guards and core/core-test/extension-test typechecks passed; the gate failed in `lint core` on untouched `src/gateway/gateway-acp-spawn-defaults.live.test.ts` (`unicorn/no-useless-spread`), outside this PR diff.
- GitHub checks re-queued after push and are still pending.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed or unreadable active runtime tool schemas are quarantined before provider/model projection instead of crashing the assistant turn before content.

Real environment tested: local linked gwt worktree for focused routed Vitest and lint/format checks; AWS Crabbox Linux runner for remote `pnpm check:changed` until the unrelated current-main lint failure.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...`; `git diff --check @{u}...HEAD`; `node scripts/changed-lanes.mjs --json`; `node scripts/check-changed.mjs --dry-run`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base @{u}`; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` in run `run_2e97ba819a4b`.

Evidence after fix: focused tests cover normal attempts, compaction attempts, quarantine logging, projection filtering, and doctor runtime checks; the remote changed gate passed guards/typechecks and failed only on an unrelated untouched gateway lint issue.

Observed result after fix: runtime projection drops bad tools, logs/diagnoses the quarantine, and preserves healthy tools so the assistant path can continue.

What was not tested: a clean full `pnpm check:changed`; the current-main lint failure above must be fixed separately or the required broad gate will remain red.
